what_it_does: It consolidates five read-only reviews into a single authoritative correction layer, revising earlier positions on reuse, concurrency, parallelism placement, injection, chat_parts, rule determinism, the claude -p floor, activation substrate, telemetry, and smaller factual items; it states that the triad docs are edited in place to match.
observations: The verdict is that the architecture is sound but the triad over-credited reuse and missed a central resource-ceiling failure.

F1 reframes every '32' concurrency claim: the resident 4B serves max_num_seqs=16 and shares KV with the 64K voice context, so the realistic co-resident ceiling is well below 16; the cap must be min(max_num_seqs - R, KV-budget / per-role-context) read from the registry.
F2 decides that parallelism lives in the cognition driver / in-turn runner, not in shared scheduler.py, keeping the app surface serial unless it opts in.
F3 reclassifies injection as net-new ref-read branch using run:// addressing and drops swarm:// because it is not a registered scheme.
F4 reclassifies chat_parts as a hot-path refactor that extracts a shared core from chat(), gated by rhm_* regression suites.
F5 requires rule evaluation to be post-barrier, deterministic, a pure function of fully-resolved address values, with banned inputs like now(), random, wave-order, or partial results.
F6 encodes the claude -p floor as an unforgeable resolve/approve-event invariant plus a regression test.
F7 reclassifies the activation substrate (G5) as fully net-new: scheduler/timer for rollups, event-hook for sense, idle-loop for background.
F8 adds a swarm telemetry batching/throttling criterion and requires atomicizing memo_set/write_provenance.

F9 collects smaller corrections: json_object vs json_schema, per-draw cache id, edge kind as CONFIRM-level contract edit, cognition.* as runtime strings, G8 extending MODEL_KNOBS, scheduler ready-set, self-description/drift homes, stale line refs, and VRAM/KV co-residence.

The net effect is a smaller reuse ledger, a measurement gate at G0, two decided design choices (parallelism in cognition driver, run:// addressing), and two asserted properties turned into enforced invariants with tests.
standouts: The central failure is the resource ceiling, which reframes all '32' concurrency assumptions.
Parallelism placement is explicitly decided: cognition driver, not scheduler.py.
Injection and chat_parts are reclassified from reuse to net-new/refactor work.
Determinism and the claude -p floor are converted from assertions into enforced invariants with tests.
conventions: Severity-ordered fold structure using F1-F9 identifiers.
Each finding uses a pattern of title, reality, revised position, and criteria/guide edits.
Cross-references guides/criteria by G# and C# identifiers and cites code line references/symbols.
States that corrections are applied in place with no versioning.
concerns: The file depends heavily on out-of-file context (guide/criteria documents, code line references) and is not self-contained.
It notes that line references are stale, which weakens precise traceability.
Some high-level assertions, such as 'the architecture is sound,' are stated without supporting evidence inside the file.
notes: Authoritative correction memo that downgrades reuse claims, fixes the 32-concurrency assumption, and turns several asserted properties into enforced invariants.
questions: What are the exact contents of the referenced guides G0-G8 and criteria C0.2, C1.1, C1.2, C1.5, C3.1, C9.2?
Which triad documents are edited in place, and how are conflicts among them resolved?
What is the precise registry format and source for max_num_seqs and KV-budget readings?
How is the 'wave-barrier' implemented, and in which module does it live?
What is the intended staging setup for the G0 measurement gate on the 0.49 voice config?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to serve as the authoritative correction layer after five read-only reviews; the file does exactly that by listing findings, revised positions, and net effect, so it matches.
apparent_themes: Honest accounting of reuse versus net-new work.
Measurement over assumption for concurrency limits.
Determinism and auditability under parallel dispatch.
Enforced invariants rather than asserted properties.
Co-residence resource awareness for local LLM serving.
intention_signals: It tries to stop the build plan from over-promising substrate reuse and from hardcoding a 32-way concurrency ceiling, while locking down design decisions and converting hand-wavy guarantees into testable invariants before implementation proceeds.
novelty: It is a meta-review fold that explicitly retracts earlier reuse classifications and reframes them as net-new or refactor work; it also treats co-resident KV-budget as the binding concurrency constraint rather than a throughput benchmark.
contribution: It contributes a corrected, severity-ordered set of design corrections and invariant definitions that a fused system would keep as the ground-truth reconciliation of round-1 review findings, especially the resource-ceiling formula, the parallelism-in-driver decision, the run:// addressing rule, and the determinism and claude -p enforcement requirements.
summary_for_embedding: R1-FOLD.md is a consolidated round-1 review correction document that revises prior positions on reuse, sets a registry-derived concurrency ceiling, places parallelism in the cognition driver, mandates run:// addressing, and converts determinism and the claude -p floor into enforced invariants.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to reconcile five read-only reviews into an authoritative, in-place correction layer that prevents over-credited reuse and hardcoded concurrency assumptions from propagating into the build plan.
